extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 10 from deep in the pack, trusting leadout man SABATINI to navigate before unleashing final sprint acceleration that gapped the field by multiple bike lengths.
tactical_patterns: KITTEL deliberately released SABATINI's wheel before final corner to avoid premature acceleration, then picked off rivals individually with timing.
Quick-Step presence forced other sprint teams to chase alone, LOTTO worked entire stage only to be dominated by KITTEL's superior speed.
Team Sky positioned FROOME at front entire stage as defensive tactic on flat stages, avoiding crashes rather than conserving energy mid-pack.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Generated extra speed burst when hitting front to prevent drafting, suggesting he can control gap size tactically not just speed
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Finished 12th despite QUICK-STEP forcing other teams to chase, indicating sprint form collapse or positioning failure
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: neutral: Team worked on front but rider missing from final sprint analysis, positioning or timing issue evident
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: negative: Had best seat on KITTEL's wheel via perfect leadout but couldn't hold contact, pure power differential exposed
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Team Sky maintained front positioning on flat stage as crash avoidance protocol, treating sprinter stages as defensive GC days
